content:
# Jauf Formation

**Summary**: A Lower Devonian sedimentary formation in Saudi Arabia, primarily composed of quartz arenites.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2001-al-ramadan|Al-Ramadan, 2001]] studied the Jauf Formation as a primary reservoir rock.

## Depositional Environment

- [[2001-al-ramadan|Al-Ramadan, 2001]] shallow marine, barrier island and associated channel sandstones

## Reservoir Properties



## Key Findings Across Sources

- Average framework grain composition is 77% quartz, 2% feldspar, and 0.1% rock fragments. ([[2001-al-ramadan|Al-Ramadan, 2001, p. 18]])

## Conflicting Interpretations Across Sources

- [[2001-al-ramadan|Al-Ramadan, 2001]] compaction, cementation (calcite, chlorite, illite, quartz), and dissolution
